pattern: Scan the words in order and return the first one that reads the same forwards and backwards; nothing qualifying means an empty string.
hints: Check each word in the given order -- the first one you find is the answer, so don't keep looking after that.
A word is a palindrome when it equals its own reverse; comparing from both ends inward works just as well as reversing it.
If you reach the end of the list without finding one, the answer is the empty string, not some other placeholder.

edge_inputs.description: no palindrome anywhere in the list
first word already is the palindrome
palindrome appears later in the list
single word, single character, trivially a palindrome
single word that is not a palindrome
near-palindrome that fails only at the very last character
even-length palindrome mixed with even-length non-palindromes
every word is a palindrome, must still return the first

stress.description: random lowercase words up to the max count and length, mostly non-palindromic with a few injected palindromes
